summary: My parents were born in Bhutan, but they had to leave in 1992 because of the civil war. The government there did not allow Nepali people to stay, so they moved to Nepal. Before they left Bhutan, my parents lived near each other and later got married in 1995.

I was born in a refugee camp in Nepal in 1997 after my parents fled Bhutan. They first stayed in the forest before the Nepalese government allowed them to build bamboo houses and provided food supplies. Later, when we left the camp, we flew from Nepal’s capital, Kathmandu, on our way to the United States.

In 2014, my family applied for resettlement in the United States because our relatives told us we could have a better life there. After a long process, we arrived in the U.S. in September 2015, first landing in Los Angeles and then moving to Colorado. A year later, we relocated to Minnesota to be closer to family.

When we first arrived in the United States in September 2015, we settled in Colorado. We stayed there for almost a year, but since we didn’t have relatives there, we decided to move to Minnesota where many of our family members lived.

After living in Colorado for about a year, my family moved to Minnesota to join our relatives. It felt good to be closer to family, and that’s where we have been since. Minnesota became our new home after the long journey from Nepal and Bhutan.
cues: move after one year in Colorado

summary: After leaving the refugee camp in Nepal, we flew to Kathmandu, the capital city, where we stayed for five days waiting for our turn to move. It was my first time seeing so many new things and it was both scary and exciting. Kathmandu was an important stop on our journey to the United States.

On our way to the United States, we had a layover in Hong Kong where we stayed for five hours waiting for our next flight. It was a long journey, and I remember feeling sick on the plane. Luckily, my mom found a Nepali speaker there who helped us communicate since she didn’t speak English.

My parents lived in a Bhutanese refugee camp in Nepal after fleeing Bhutan in 1992. At first, they had no proper shelter and stayed in the forest until the Nepalese government allowed them to build bamboo houses and provided food supplies. I was born there in 1997, and it was the place where our family started before moving to the United States.
